reviews.title: Don’t stay here if you drive
reviews.text:
If you are intending to drive pls don’t stay at this hotel! Parking lots were super limited for a hotel of this size. We couldn’t park in the hotel for 2
Out of 3 days despite being guests and claims of having parking lots on site in the facilities. We had to park further away which was so inconvenient during the rainy season. 
The lift and facilities were severely over crowded. Laundry machines were fully used and had to wait until the dead of the night to do laundry. Sauna for the ladies were constantly at a crowded situation. Not gonna stay here again.
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying at APA Hotel & Resort Yokohama Bay Tower.

We apologize for the inconvenience caused by the parking lot.
As you pointed out, our parking lot has limited space, and it may not be available during busy periods.
We will strive to improve our services so that our guests will not experience any inconvenience in the future.

We also apologize for any inconvenience caused by the congestion in the elevators, laundry machines, and large bath.  
Regarding the laundry machines, we are considering expanding the coin laundry facilities to make them more comfortable to use.  
Additionally, if a customer who has exceeded the time limit has not yet retrieved their laundry, please inform the front desk staff, and we will retrieve it for you.  
We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause, but we hope you will make use of this service as well.

We sincerely appreciate your taking the time to post a review despite your busy schedule. 
We look forward to welcoming you

reviews.title: Miserable tiny room in a money hungry establishment
reviews.positives: Nothing really likeable about this Hotel
reviews.negatives: We had booked in an extra night to extend the previous booking of our Tour Company. Instead of letting us move from one room to another, they forced us to check out, then wait all day to 3 pm before we could check in again. Then to check in early by two hours at 1 pm they changed us an extra 2000 yen, even though room was actually ready. Talk about money hungry. Then the Room, even by Japanese standards was miniscule. Worst ever experience at a hotel in Japan, wouldn't recommend. Stay at your own risk. For what guests pay for, you could get a much better & cleaner accommodation at a home stay. Never, ever, ever staying at a APA branch again. Lesson learnt.

reviews.title: Unbearably hot
reviews.text:
The hotel itself is lovely. The rooms are compact, modern and clean. But the hotel has set a policy that heating is centrally fixed. And it was hot — close to 30 degrees. Although you can turn the air con off, it takes hours before the temperature comes down. Since you cannot open the window nor lower the temperature yourself, you are stuck with the central setting. The hotel staff can lend you a small fan but all that does is blow the hot air around.  I had an uncomfortable night and got very little sleep. 

Generally, I like the APA chain. But my advice is NOT to stay here. You will pay top dollar ( or yen) for a deeply uncomfortable stay. 

To the APA Yokohama, please discontinue your policy of central tower heating. Allow guest to set their own comfort level. I have not stayed in a hotel - in Japan or elsewhere - that adopts a practice as silly. 

Sorry for the negative feedback. But I genuinely regretted staying with this hotel. And, it goes without saying, I will not be back. .

reviews.title: Definitely won’t be back here!
reviews.text:
The hotel is over packed. Good location near the bay if you want to go shopping in the mall nearby (before 9pm). However, it has over 30 floors and many rooms per floor, with only one public bath. The public bath is always busy, especially for women. You have to queue for lockers, again queue for shower, after, again for soaking into those tubs…again for hair dryer…for 20 mins of hot tub, prepare to wait for 30 mins or more. Wait! You have to queue for elevators to go back up (remember it’s 4th floor, you have to wait for over 10 mins to fit into the elevator half full to go upstairs)…

It’s over the capacity! 

Also, as overseas customers, I’m used to give the passports directly instead of completing things on machines. However, you have to use the machine to finish the check-in (also long wait for check-in) it would be better to have human to quicken the whole process!

Luggage storage. Only same day luggage. I wanted to leave my luggage here to pick up the second day once returns from a 24 hours trip nearby but hotel said no next day pick-up. Probably because this hotel is definitely over its capacity for customers (but usually hotels in Japan offer luggage storage for days or longer). Another reason I think it’s not worth it. 

FYI, I booked two rooms, US200 per room per night during late March (booking made in early Jan)

I would not recommend this hotel for the experience!

reviews.title: GO WITH A BETTER HOTEL, VERY UNCOMFORTABLE.
reviews.negatives: Americans Beware! I looked at this hotel and thought it was a beautiful view for the price. This has got to the the worst hotel experience I have had. Firstly the hotel room for the price is TINY! The bed feels so hard and stiff like you are sleeping on a floor. The pillows they give you feel like thin bath mats, So the sleeping experience was not comfortable. The TV has a screen mirroring function that did not work , so we were unable to watch anything while to fall asleep. The hotel offers a private onsen and air bath which you cannot use if you have TATTOOS  Which is not listed on booking.com. On our last night there after we contemplated just going home to go to sleep, we decided to stay and the whole hotel was woken up at 2:30A.M in the morning due to a false fire alarm, because if you shower and leave the door open because the room is so tiny it sets off the fire alarm. Our friend had to go to work at 8 a.m that morning and was woken up and couldn't go back to sleep. Please go with a better hotel and don't let the price win you over!! I will not ever be returning or recommend this hotel to anyone. The overall look of the hotel tricks you into what you will be getting when staying there.

reviews.title: WORST STAY!!!!
reviews.text:
APA Hotel is very accessible and conveniently located near major shopping areas.

However, my overall experience was not comfortable. The room is extremely small, which made it difficult to relax. The bathroom design was also uncomfortable, as the glass walls make it feel too exposed and lacking in privacy.

During check-in, there was a long queue. Since I do not speak Japanese, I politely approached the receptionist to confirm if I was in the correct line. Unfortunately, the staff on duty was not accommodating and came across as rude, despite my respectful approach. Coming from a customer service background, I understand processes and was willing to wait, but the interaction could have been handled more professionally.

Overall, the space feels cramped, and the experience did not meet expectations. I would not recommend staying here, especially if comfort and service are priorities.

reviews.title: Worst experience in Japan
reviews.text: I just hope that it will be cleared on the website or the listing that the elementary students are considered adults. We were being charged for a room so my 8yr old can sleep there. By herself? So i upgraded our room to a triple room and paid extra instead. And also the staff ARE NOT WELCOMING at all. From the luggage drop off to checkin. Nobody is helpful, no one was welcoming. Especially a guy named Tanaka. He was pressing the keyboard hard and was not helpful at all when i was explaining to him the issue i had with the room keys. Imagine we book two rooms and received 4 keys. We got one room key inserted and working on each rooms however, one of them is not working and when I request for him to get it replaced. He said that the two keys that i have in my hand are for one room. Then how did we enter the other room if its not meant for it. Isnt that a security issue? My experience with him and with this APA is the worst we had in Japan! And we’ve been living here for 6 years! I will never use APA anymore anywhere.
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying at APA Hotel & Resort Yokohama Bay Tower.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience caused regarding luggage storage.
We will thoroughly review staff training and strive to ensure greater satisfaction for all our guests moving forward.

We also apologize for the trouble regarding the room key.
All room keys at our hotel share the same design.
As you had a reservation for two rooms, it is possible that the keys for both rooms became mixed up.
We will improve our future announcements to ensure staff provide sufficient guidance when handing out room keys.

We will continue striving to enhance our services to ensure your satisfaction.
We sincerely appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback.
We look forward to welcoming you back in the future.

Front Desk, Ishikawa

reviews.title: Worst check-in experience / staff Ever
reviews.text:
Worst Check-In Experience Ever

I recently had the most frustrating check-in experience at this hotel. Despite having several front desk clerks, the hotel insisted on using a check-in machine, resulting in a long, slow-moving line. The staff were unhelpful and indifferent to our concerns.

We specifically requested non-smoking rooms when booking, but upon arrival, we were assigned smoking rooms. When we brought this to the attention of the front desk clerk, we were told that no non-smoking rooms were available, despite seeing two available online. 

We spent two hours trying to resolve this issue with the hotel staff, but it was all in vain. The experience was stressful and absolutely not worth it. I would not recommend staying at this hotel.

reviews.title: I won't be back, this hotel is not for me, staff very unfriendly and unhelpful.
reviews.positives: I arrived in Japan early morning and requested an early check-in, as I knew I would be tired. Most major hotels that I normally use will usually allow this as a sign of good faith with the aim of helping the customer. Of course, if the hotel is 100% full I understand that this is not possible but when I arrived, I asked about my request and just a very flat response of no check-in until 3 pm. Whilst sitting in the hotel lobby I watched a couple check in early because the man insisted on it in a firm manner, perhaps the staff were worried about his demeanour.  On the day I was leaving, I found the check-out time was earlier than I expected and as I was not well, I rang the front desk and asked for a n extension. I was given just two hrs. When I checked out, I was charged an extra 2000 yen for the late checkout. I asked for this to be waived as I was a customer of 4 nights and thought that the hotel might indeed help, but the answer was a flat no. Also, when I had to go to a wedding, I asked the front if they could assist me in changing some older notes for newer-looking notes (10,000 yen notes x 3), as I wanted to gift my friend with crisper notes than what I had. Again, I was given a flat no. It seems to me that the.  staff either do not care or have no flexibility in order to help out hotel guests. It was VERY annoying and VERY poor service.
reviews.negatives: The room was VERY small. The staff had no desire to truly consider or serve the guests, they were absolutely inflexible with ALL requests. The carpet had old food on the floor, not properly cleaned.

reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying at APA Hotel & Resort Yokohama Bay Tower.
Our hotel features a large communal bath on the 4th floor. You can check its occupancy status at any time via your room TV.
We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused regarding the air conditioning. Our hotel operates a centralized heating and cooling system. Therefore, while quantities are limited, we do offer portable air conditioners and circulators for rent to guests who request them. reviews.text: I stayed for 2 nights and the room smells like sweat. Tried to use that deodorizer but it still smelled like sweat in the bathroom and bed. Smelled musky. I called 10 mins in since I didn’t want to spend my whole morning waiting on laundry. Front desk did not come. After 30 mins has gone by I called again. Still did not arrive. I was able to wait for other to finish and when I came back to grab my clothes. The same clothes was still there. Not to mentioned that when shipping our luggage’s to our next destination they just gave us the papers and we had to translate everything ourselves.  Of all the hotels I stayed at APA would have to be the worst in service and room cleanliness.

reviews.title: Decent Accommodations
reviews.text: If you are looking for a quick place to stay while visiting Yokohama, this place will meet your needs. The accommodations were decent for the amount we paid. However there were a few downsides to staying here. First, the location is a far walking distance from the station we transferred from Shin-Yokohama. Second, the staff were not helpful and gave mixed information when trying to book a taxi. Then there weren’t clear rules that the bathhouse were not tattoo friendly so it was a waste to pay and not use any of the amenities. The check in process was quick but felt like I left with unanswered questions. Lastly, despite there being 6 elevators somehow only a few would get used so be prepared to stop at 5+ floors before getting to your stop.

reviews.title: No relaxation at this "resort".
reviews.positives: Location and view from our room.
reviews.negatives: Crowds of people. Too big a hotel. Not allowed to use pool unless you booked a 90 minute slot in advance and paid 2000Yen PER PERSON!! (It was booked out anyway). Check in was a line as long as at the airport with 10 desks and the queue snaked all around the foyer in a roped-off area. This was at 6.30pm. The check-in staff couldn't speak any English (ok, I can't speak any Japanese) but this was the first hotel of 5 that this happened. They also made you use a machine to do auto check-in and it didn't recognise my Booking.com booking number. The restaurants in the hotel were not appealing and it had a cruise ship atmosphere. We had hoped to have our last 2 days in Japan relaxing by the pool at a "resort hotel". Nothing relaxing or classy about this hotel.

reviews.title: Stay away if you have Tattoos and want to use the Onsen.
reviews.positives: The location is great for wanting to check out the Yokohama area on foot. There is no shortage of shops and restaurants within walking distance. The hotel is very secure and there are two convient stores on the 4th floor. There is very limited English TV channels to pick from, but you can cast to the TV from your phone or tablet to watch Netflix or YouTube.
reviews.negatives: I booked this hotel for two reasons. 1) location. 2) it has several quasi-natural Komeiseki Onsen. So I was shocked when I was about to enter the Onsen after a long day of walking around Yokohama, only to be told I could not enter because I have a few tattoos. Nowhere on the website when I booked my 3 night stay did I see anything about people with tattoos not allowed in the Onsen. If I had, I would not have booked it. I have stayed as several other APA brand hotels in Japan, and have never had this issue. The worst part was that the hotel employee told me that tattoos are bad, and that they had to protect the Japanese customers. Apparently, western customers are not important at this hotel. Especially those with tattoos. Being that we are no longer in the 1950's, and tattoos are very common not only with westerners, but also many younger Japanese. Another issue is that the toilet is right up against the wall in the small bathroom. You basically have to side on the toilet sideways. Very poor design. The entire hotel HVAC system if tied together. So if the heating is on, as was the case, there is no way to turn the A/C on in your room. In my case, it was uncomfortably hot in my room. I kept the HVAC turned off my entire stay, and it was still hot at night while trying to sleep. Lastly, I had a rental car for the middle night of my stay. After spending the day in Kamakura and upon my return to the hotel, there was no available parking. I ended up having to park at an offsite area and walk to the hotel.

reviews.title: Such a pain to find a parking lot
reviews.text: The hotel is not having enough parking lots for guests. They should have preserved lots only for staying guests and only staying guests can park cars overnight. The hotel is convenient and has lots of amenities but is crowded with families and kids.

reviews.title: Looks are deceiving. Not comfortable. Don’t waste your money.
reviews.positives: Location is the best part of this hotel. Walking distance to many places like Cup Noodle Museum, train station, and restaurants.
reviews.negatives: It was EXTREMELY hot. The air was set to heat and had no option for cold air conditioning. There was a sign in the lobby that they won’t turn on the cool air due to the outside temperature but yet the hallways and other areas were nice and cool. My whole family (including a toddler and infant) had to sleep with no clothes on to be slightly comfortable. I asked the staff for a fan and they said they could not accommodate. Another thing I didn’t like was the food. It was ¥2900 per person but the food was not tasty— Japanese or the western food. Lastly, the hallways were always crowded because the housekeepers would park their large carts of sheets, toiletries, and towels all in the hallway— all day, every day. They would gather large trash bags and leave them in the hallway. Staff was not friendly and helpful. The hotel charges ¥1000 for early check in. I literally checked in 35 minutes early and they still wanted to charge?! I had a crying baby and toddler! Definitely not a family friendly hotel and not coming back ever.

reviews.title: Grand hotel, tiny room,  not tattoo-friendly
reviews.text: You get the 4-5 star hotel impression as soon as you walk into the lobby with its grandeur. Its centrally located with only a short walk to most of the tourist attractions. Our room had a fantastic view over the bay. The negatives?   Given the  hotel stature I was surprised that so few  staff at  reception/information/check-in had trouble with the most basic requests in English. I've traveled a lot of Japan and stayed in big and small accommodation in big cities and small towns and rarely encountered this problem.  The room we got had an amazing view but was small even by Japanese standards. Big enough to hold a double bed but virtually no floor space  (a small strip to navigate from the bed to the bathroom and the front door). They have a no-tattoo policy in the 'Grand Spa' that they offer for free to uninked guests (if you only have a small tattoo they will offer a small patch at reception to cover up).  Even the discounted price was too expensive for what was delivered. Wont be back

i was given a smoking room despite indicating on my agoda requests that i wanted a non smoking one. I was told i would have to pay more for a non-smoking one, so already i started my stay on a very disappointing and unpleasant note. 

The facilities are great outside but the room itself is small, the windows cannot be opened and you cannot adjust the central air-conditioning, all of which worsens the cigarette smoke problem. 

checkout time is 10am which is earlier than most other hotels which allow 11am checkouts. 

I have stayed in other APA hotel properties before and generally enjoyed them. This is the first time i was really disappointed with the quality of my accommodation.  This property is easily the worst APA hotel i have stayed at.

reviews.title: Basically a business hotel.
reviews.text: I booked an APA hotel for the first time in a while, but since I'm not an APA member, I had to fill out various forms on the portal when entering the hotel, which was very bothersome. I understand that there are many foreign travelers and businessmen staying, but I was tired from work and found it very unpleasant at check-in. The room was very clean and tidy. However, the TV remote didn't work, and when I tried to contact the front desk, there was no contact information anywhere, so I reluctantly took the remote to the front desk. I was told that the remote wouldn't work unless pointed in the right direction—something anyone could understand—and after waiting for about 5–6 minutes, the staff accompanied me to the room and, after operating the remote, found that the batteries were dead. The staff told me to "call the front desk," but when I mentioned that there was no information anywhere, I was informed that, in reality, the phone number for the front desk was supposed to be written on the receiver, but it wasn't there, which is why I had to go to the front desk to convey the issue. Even if the staff had no bad intentions, shouldn't they be aware of such things from cleaning the rooms every day? Around 1 to 3 AM, there were probably Chinese people (since they were speaking Chinese) walking and talking loudly. The walls are thick, but the doors are quite thin, so I could hear them. I think the breakfast had the most variety of any hotel I've stayed at so far. The taste was also good. Given the hotel rates are somewhat high, I felt like it might be acceptable to stay somewhere else. The location is close to Bashamichi, and both Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse and Minato Mirai are within walking distance, so I think the location is good.

Breakfast service is chaotic, information about the food ingredients is poor and there are queues to enter the restaurant. There are no food options for vegans, people allergic to gluten and vegetarians. Any simple service is paid, even ordering a taxi or loosing your room key. The hotel laundry is very expensive, forcing the guest to use the coin laundry which has huge queues.

reviews.title: Check in 3pm check out 10 am!
reviews.text:
Check in is too late and check out too early - this hotel is not convenient for overseas guests. Check in process involved huge, huge line up (like an immigration line at airport). Never experienced this before in a hotel. 
Guest waiting had nowhere to sit. No water offered to jet lagged guests. No management discretion to let guest in early if room was ready. 
If you have large luggage there is no space in room.
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying at APA Hotel & Resort <Yokohama Bay Tower>.

We are very sorry for the inconvenience caused by the congestion at check-in and the lack of waiting space.
We expect check-in to be crowded, especially on weekends, holidays and during events in the neighbourhood.
Our staff are working hard to ensure a smooth check-in process, and we would be grateful for your understanding.
Please note that you can leave your luggage with us before check-in, so please make use of this next time you stay with us.

In terms of room space, the rooms are also compact and suitcases can be stored under the bed. We will take your feedback into account to improve our services in the future.

reviews.title: Check out at 10 am?
reviews.text: Check in process was very impersonal, all computerized no explanations, and NO mention of a 10:00 am check-out!  The room was tiny, smaller than any cruise ship cabin, and the double bed was against the wall.  Bathroom is again tiny.  Probably our biggest disappointment was laying in bed at 9:50 am, thinking about getting up, we planned on checking out by 11, the TV comes on and tells us that check out time is 10, and there's a 2000/hr. Charged for late checkout!  After paying top price for this hotel, and checking in after midnight, and paying $56 cdn for 2 cappuccinos and a microwaved pancake thing (just 1) for breakfast at a hotel Cafe, the day before, we just felt ripped off.   The room was clean, and we'll supplied, but there was no room for suitcases even other than under the bed. We also discovered that they don't make up the room during the day, unless you put out the make up room magnet on the door.  We did get clean towels left hanging beside the door, but the bed was never made, nor trash taken out.  Also the pool was dirty, and closed. The check out time was not posted on the door, nor was it on my booking confirmation.   I have stayed in hotels every night for the past 6 months, and I unfortunately had higher expectations for this one

reviews.title: It is an ideal location for sightseeing in Yokohama.
reviews.text:
I used it for the first time during New Year's. The exterior is luxurious, but inside it's an APA business hotel, is my impression. I think it's sufficient for staying casually as a single person or with a family, but if you are expecting a somewhat richer stay, you might be a bit taken aback.

<Good points>
- There are two convenience stores (FamilyMart) inside the hotel, which is convenient, and there are also dining options available. 
  ⇒ It's helpful to be able to buy drinks and food at midnight. There are also travel-sized toiletries available for unexpected needs. I was able to purchase New Year's soba and traditional Japanese sweets (nerikiri).
- There are ice machines and microwaves (on designated floors).
- Hand soap is made by Saraya.
  ⇒ I often bring my own soap because hotel hand soaps don’t agree with me, but since I use Saraya at home, it was gentle on my hands and comfortable.
- Fireworks can be seen from a nearby bridge.
  ⇒ Even without being on a high floor, you can see a bit if you go outside.
- It's nice that there is something to throw on over your clothes.
  ⇒ Especially in winter, I want to have something to wear over my outfit, but it can be cumbersome, so having a shawl or similar item is unexpectedly helpful. The hallway to the room inside the hotel was cold.

<Bad points>
- Check-in and the bath were extremely crowded.
  ⇒ I'm sure many others have written about this, but as it is a large hotel, there are many people. Specifically, check-in (in the evening) was extremely crowded, and although the hotel staff provided guidance on web check-in (distributing papers), being my first time, I didn’t know how it worked and since I had pre-paid through Agoda, I could check in instantly with the app. I wish there had been an explanation like, "If you've pre-paid, you can check in quickly with the app." In the end, I waited in line for about 40 minutes unnecessarily, which was exhausting.
  Check-out is smooth as it just involves returning the card key.
  Also, perhaps due to the large number of guests, the handling of stored luggage was a bit rough (snatched away), which made me a bit anxious, but the contents were safe.
  I understand the congestion level of the large public bath, but since there are so many guests, although there are enough showers, the sinks after bathing required waiting in line, so it didn't feel very relaxing.
- No additional breakfast available due to full reservations.
  ⇒ This is unavoidable, but when I tried to add breakfast to my stay, it was fully booked. I thought it would be fine since it’s open until 2 PM, but it seems that making a reservation in advance is safer.
- The height of the bed and the room layout were somewhat lacking…?
  ⇒ It likely depends on the room, but the bed was set high enough to store luggage underneath, which was quite tough on tired legs (it felt like climbing up). I don’t want to put my luggage on the floor, so I wondered if this storage was necessary…? 
  Additionally, the coat hooks and desk were far from the bed, so every time I needed to do something, I had to get up, which made the layout feel odd and inconvenient… I found myself more concerned about this than the room size.
- The toilet seat was cold.
  ⇒ It does provide hot water, but it wasn't a heated toilet seat…
- Only one face towel was provided.
  ⇒ I think this might be due to the eco trend, but since it’s a style to take the room towels to the large public bath, having only one face towel was a bit inconvenient for washing my face in the morning… 
  (I think they would provide more if you ask at the front desk...)
- The welcome drink was simply coffee and green tea (no black tea) in paper cups.

Thank you very much for staying at APA Hotel & Resort Yokohama Bay Tower.

We are delighted to receive your kind words regarding our location. Our hotel is situated in the Yokohama Minato Mirai area, offering beautiful views characteristic of the port city of Yokohama. The surrounding area features numerous restaurants and tourist attractions, with the view from Bannkoku Bridge being particularly popular among our guests. We hope you will take the opportunity to explore the surrounding sights during your next stay.

We also appreciate your feedback regarding pricing. We will use this as valuable reference for future improvements.
Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts despite your busy schedule.
Our entire staff sincerely looks forward to welcoming you back.

reviews.title: Cockroach crawled on me in my sleep
reviews.text: I think my title explains the experience of my stay. There was a cockroach in our room and I felt something crawl on me and I woke up to find a cockroach on my arm. It was not a small one either. I called the concierge and let them know my situation (thankfully I knew some Japanese because they could not understand any English). A nice young bell boy came up to our room with a can of bug spray and a towel. I explained that even if you find the bug and kill it I will not be staying in the same room again. It took some frustrated explanation and pressure in order for them to allow us to switch rooms.  We ended up losing our original view (that we paid extra for) but at that point I was too exhausted and defeated to make another complaint. Needless to say I was not comfortable for the rest of my stay there and would probably never stay there again.

The heating was an issue as there is a control panel in the room to adjust the temperature but in fact it doesn’t work at all and central heating is on full time while the temperature is warm around 12-20 degrees, I asked front desk if they could adjust but the answer was no, unfortunately the window doesn’t open as well so the heat is just trapped and makes it very hard to get a good nite sleep especially when I was having trouble breathing, I had to open the door and use the bin to keep it open so can get some air into the room. reviews.title: a very nice room and nice property but the stay was spoiled by an uncomfortable room temperature.
reviews.positives: Excellent location, amazing view, a wide variety of amenities and conveniences at the location, very near the local metro system, near shopping and tourist sites
reviews.negatives: air conditioning system was poor especially during a very warm period. There was no information in the room about where to find the A/C controls which meant the room temperature kept climbing up and up to 28 degrees C / 82F. There was more information available about how to operate the toilet and bidet than the A/C controls. I had to speak with a member of staff at reception using google translate and he informed me that the controls could be found behind the pillows on the bed. Even with the A/C on for the entire evening the room temperature never fell below 22 C which was still a little too warm.

reviews.title: Facility didn't meet expectations
reviews.text: Overall pretty bad experience at this specific hotel. The walk there was quite far from the train station, and then the elevators going from the lobby and laundry room to our rooms were PACKED. Had to wait 10 minutes just to get on the elevator because of people going up and down to the SPA on the 4th floor. The amenities were nice and also the location was right next to the coastal shopping and dining areas. Another bad thing about the hotel stay was that we didn't have AC. The room was the smallest out of the 4 different hotels we stayed at during our trip, and the only one without AC. We had to ask the front desk for a small fan. This was during March, when the weather was cooler, so I could only imagine staying at thw hotel during summer. No thank you. Overall very disappointed in this hotel stay. Would not recommend.

reviews.positives:
Overall this is a pretty good quality hotel ; The beds are alright, the view is good, the room & facilities are clean.
The location is great, you can get to yokohama station in about 5 min and tokyo in ~40 ish.
reviews.negatives:
You may notice that my rating isn't that high and that's because of the centralized AC.
You can't control the temperature of the AC, only the hotel can.
It was 23.5 in my room with only a computer on. If you were to be multiple people in and/or take a shower the temp would ramp up even more. But since the hotel decided to put the AC in "heat" mode i had no way of refreshing the room.
I had to keep the door open and get some fresh air from the hallway. This is extremely embarrassing.
AC is a standard equipment in japan and it is to be expected, my night were ruined thanks to that.

reviews.title: スタッフの対応が最悪
reviews.text:
ホテルを今日チェックアウトしました。その際に関西に数日行った後にまたこのホテル帰って来て泊まる予定があるので大きいスーツケースを預かってもらえますか？とフロントに聞いたら荷物を預かる予約がないと駄目だと言われ戸惑ってしまいました。このホテルにはもう何回も泊まっていて同じサービスを利用した事があるのに荷物を数日預かってもらうには事前予約が必要になったのか？と不思議に思っていたら…

よく話を聞かずにまた数日後の宿泊予約なしだと決めつけ宿泊予約をとってから荷物を預かって欲しいなら戻ってこいと言われました。私が不快に感じたのは受付のホテルマンの態度が威圧的だった事、きちんと数日後の宿泊予約の確認がとれても態度が人を小馬鹿にしている態度でびっくり‼️
決めつける前に人の話を聞いてほしかった。

正直今まではこのホテルではいい経験しかなくだから何度もリピートして使ってきたのに…きちんとカスタマーサービスを教育されてないホテルマンが居たのが残念だ…
あんな態度を取られた後にもしスーツケースがなくなっていたら2度とこのホテルは使わない。

reviews.text: Rooms are tiny but eco friendly as they don’t offer room service everyday only the last day before I checked out.  Guess that’s how they keep prices low.  Pool is offered only for a fee but the “onsen” was nice.  Overall it’s what you pay for and it’s adequate given its location and proximity to the Ferris wheel.

reviews.title: Good location.   Be prepared for the small rooms.
reviews.positives: Nearest station was about 5 minute walk.  Bashamichi station had many escalators and elevators so street access was good with luggage.  Hotel appearance was deceiving.  Outside looked like it would be a resort type experience.  Spa was excellent, big, and separate outdoor pool was available.  Inside the room was tiny.  Good size bed but only could enter from one side. Other 3 sides were next to a wall.  View was great.  Now for the bad.  One day was my wife's birthday so we decided to try the breakfast.  We tried to reserve one day before but concierge said no time was available for our day.  Back in the room I called front desk with same results.   I tried to make reservations with a third party reservation site and succeeded.  On our reservation day we decided to try eating one  hour early since we were hungry.  Restaurant was empty, maybe 10 customers so no problem eating early.  When our reservation time came around many people started to show up.  Go figure.  Checking out was a problem.  Print out when we checked in stated 2 different checkout times, 1000 and 1100.  1014 check out day, someone called and said check out was 1000, sounded very rude and irritated.  At checkout I showed front desk the 1100 check out time.  Still said check out was 1000.  APA Hotel needs to edit their English language.
reviews.negatives: Better coordination or communication between staff and restaurant.  Making the rooms bigger is impossible.

reviews.title: I was disappointed by this hotel.
reviews.positives: Hotel is clean and shiny. Housekeeping people are friendly and professional. Breakfast area is bright and comfortable. Breakfast was pretty decent. Location of the hotel is good. It takes 10 minutes to walk to Cable area, Red warehouse, Ferry areas with beautiful views.
reviews.negatives: The reception (check-in area) are very unprofessional, had no good intention of communication, and not helpful at all. When asking about tour buses, no information at all. At the checking out point, I wanted to drop off a "prepaid Wifi returning package". They refused to take it. I went to Japan a few times. I always dropped of the prepaid Wifi package at hotel  when checked out before going to airport. Dropping off a mail is a common service every hotel does. This APA hotel told me that I had to go to post office to do it myself.  I guess I will choose other hotels next trip to Yokohama.

reviews.title: Potentially a Wonderful Hotel but Needs to Fix Some Major Issues.
reviews.positives: This is a very high quality hotel in appearance. It has many facilities and is in a good location. The staff are friendly. The onsen is good and I like the shops inside the hotel and the restaurants and bars.
reviews.negatives: The breakfast has a good selection of food but the restaurant is very badly organized. There is no one way system so it feels very unsafe and dangerous as people are moving in all different directions. There were also no windows open to aid ventilation and prevent the spread of covid 19. Also, there were often dirty dishes left on the tables and not cleared away for half an hour or more. Also, the fire alarm went off twice and one of these times was 2 o clock in the morning which was terrifying. Luckily a false alarm but they need to fix the system. I also wish they had a cold pool in the onsen.

reviews.title: Good for solo and healthy travellers
reviews.text:
First off, the self check-in lanes are practical and quick for solo travellers or younger groups but might be inconvenient and not senior-friendly. 

Taxis are also hard to catch unless you call for them (which adds a ¥500 service fee) so I wouldn't recommend if you're traveling with anyone who has challenges walking distances.  Otherwise, it's in a good spot close to some touristy places and malls. 

Overall, the rooms and ammenities are clean and basic but there is a Family Mart inside the hotel that's very convenient for other necessities. :)

reviews.title: Fire alarm incident
reviews.text:
I would give this hotel a higher review except for 1 issue.  A fire alarm went off in the middle of the night and the announcements were made in Japanese with no indication of what to do for those who don't speak or understand Japanese. Thankfully it was a false alarm, but very little guidance was given and for heavy sleepers, we basically slept through the alarm.

It's such a big hotel with so many room, that I guess it's hard to keep track of people. 

Otherwise. The facilities are great. Close to the transportation and walking distance to the cup noodle museum. The "onsen" style spa rooms were well kept and clean. Liked that family marts were conveniently located inside the hotel as well. Staff was ok, didn't have to interact much, so I can't say much more than that. Overall, it was great for the value during low season.

reviews.title: Not my style
reviews.text: I should have read the reviews more closely for this hotel. It is a huge property and the rooms are extremely small. While I managed to make it work, it was an uncomfortable stay for me. The heat was too much in my room, even after turning it off. The under bed storage for my luggage was barely big enough to keep everything put away. The room was somewhat dusty but otherwise seemed clean. The staff was friendly and did provide me with a fan so I could cool off the room enough to sleep. This is the kind of hotel I would only sleep in, not relax and feel comfortable in the room. It was very inexpensive but I would have paid more for a bit more comfort. Not the best way to end my trip to Japan.

reviews.title: New Year’s weekend
reviews.text: I stayed three days at the APA resort in Yokohama. The location is wonderful however it took me an hour to check in because everyone has to wait until 3 PM to check in. I even waited till 4 PM and I still had to wait in line for an entire hour before I could  reach the check  in desk to retrieve my key and I had already paid online.. They need to stagger the check in times or have automated online check in from your phones or something. After arriving in Yokohama at noon, walking around and waiting until 4 PM to check in,, thinking the crowd would have died down, I was exhausted by the time I got into my room at 5 PM.  The public bath was nice but very crowded as well as the convenient store located on the fourth floor of the hotel. I found the convenient  store outside of the hotel much more convenient and less crowded. My room was small, but I expected that, it was clean, but the bed was one of the hardest mattresses I have ever slept on.   I was in pain after 3 nights on that bed.
reviews.response:

Front Desk　Shibata

reviews.title: It was beautiful and good.
reviews.text: The location is good and close to the station, making it easy to sightseeing on foot. There was a long line starting from the check-in time of 3 PM, but there were many check-in kiosks available and I was able to check in faster than expected. I used the convenience store inside the facility after 9 PM, but there was a long line due to guests coming out of the baths and those returning from events. I purchased a late-night snack and used the microwave, but there was also a long line there. The elevator to return to my room was only operating one car, resulting in a long line. When people with strollers and suitcases boarded, I had to let them go first, making it difficult to return to my room. This was my second time using the facility, and I have a strong impression of long lines from both visits. I believe I could have a more comfortable stay by adjusting the check-in time, using the large public bath and doing shopping at an earlier time, and avoiding using the elevator after 9 PM.

reviews.title: Very clean hotel in a fantastic location!
reviews.text:
We had a very pleasant stay at APA Hotel & Resort Yokohama Bay Tower. The location is excellent, with World Porters and Bashamichi Station both within a 5-minute walk. A nearby bus stop also made it easy to reach Yamashita Park and the Hikawa Maru museum ship (220 yen per ride).
The hotel is large, modern, and very clean, with several restaurants, a cake shop, and two 24-hour FamilyMart stores offering tax-free shopping. There is also a public bath, which seemed popular with guests especially at night time.
We stayed on the 34th floor and found the elevators fast and efficient. The room was compact but clean, and a yukata set was provided for use inside the hotel. They also offer free luggage storage before check-in and after check-out, which was a huge plus for us. Staff were friendly, helpful, and spoke English well.
The downsides were mainly related to space and bathroom design. The bathroom was quite small, and the step into it was fairly high, which may be inconvenient for some guests, especially older travellers or those with mobility concerns. The room itself was also quite compact, making it difficult to fully open large suitcases (but that is typical with Japanese business hotels). Also we wished there were more skincare amenities provided. We only received one skincare set during our 4-night stay and had to request more from the lobby when we ran out.
Overall, this is a great value hotel with an excellent location, good facilities, and easy access to Yokohama's main attractions. We would happily stay here again.

reviews.title: The best large public bath.
reviews.text: The best way to relieve fatigue is definitely to soak in hot water. This hotel has various baths such as open-air baths, jet baths, and bubble baths that made my daily fatigue disappear as if it were a lie. The buffet-style breakfast is also plentiful and satisfying. I have enjoyed many travels, including overseas, but the cost performance here is of the highest standard. The location is also close to the station and offers good access, making it easy to plan trips to Chinatown or Enoshima.
